summary: The Multi-Location Operations Platform rollout faces misalignment between corporate standardization goals and regional operational realities. Regional managers demand local customization due to differing regulatory and site-specific data fields. Compounding this, there is no ownership defined for employee training, and the rollout sequencing (which locations launch when) remains undecided.
topBlockers: Misalignment between standardized corporate workflows and customized regional requirements.
Differing data schemas and required fields across locations, causing reporting fragmentation.
Undefined training ownership and rollout sequencing plan.

risks.title: Corporate standardization vs regional customization conflict

evidence.excerpt: Corporate expects standardized workflows. Regional managers require local customization.

risks.businessImpact: High chance of regional mutiny or poor system adoption. If corporate forces a rigid template, regional managers will use offline workarounds (e.g. spreadsheets), rendering corporate data analytics inaccurate.
risks.reasoning: A core conflict between top-down data normalization goals and bottom-up operational variance.
risks.recommendedAction: Implement a 'core template plus extensions' model. Allow local configurations for up to 20% of data fields, while keeping the core 80% standardized.
risks.validationQuestion: What specific regional workflows are legally required to differ from the corporate template?

risks.title: Incompatible local data schema requirements

evidence.excerpt: Required data fields differ between locations.

risks.businessImpact: System configuration blocks or reporting failures. Database syncs will fail if mandatory fields are null or formatted differently across locations.
risks.reasoning: Missing field mapping dependencies between regional legacy systems and the new standardized data warehouse schema.
risks.recommendedAction: Map the data schemas for all regions. Standardize common fields and create local flex fields for location-specific data compliance.
risks.validationQuestion: Can we map the differing local data fields into a flexible JSON metadata field in the database?

risks.title: Undefined training ownership

evidence.excerpt: Training ownership and rollout sequencing are undefined.

risks.businessImpact: Failure of user adoption. Site managers will not know who is responsible for training materials, resulting in untrained employees and high error rates.
risks.reasoning: Critical enablement tasks are unassigned, leading to confusion and deferred accountability.
risks.recommendedAction: Assign a Lead Enablement Manager to own training. Appoint regional 'super users' at each location to manage peer training.
risks.validationQuestion: Who will fund the training hours for the regional staff?

risks.title: Lack of rollout sequencing plan

evidence.excerpt: Training ownership and rollout sequencing are undefined.

risks.businessImpact: Operational chaos. Attempting a 'big bang' launch across all locations simultaneously will overwhelm the support desk.
risks.reasoning: Rollout phases are not sequenced, meaning resources cannot be concentrated where needed.
risks.recommendedAction: Design a phased rollout schedule. Start with a single pilot location in Month 1, followed by a regional wave in Month 2, and global launch in Month 3.
risks.validationQuestion: Which location is best suited (most stable operations) to serve as our initial pilot site?

stakeholders.name: Corporate Leadership
goals: Standardize reporting across all global divisions
Reduce IT maintenance costs by consolidating platforms
conflicts: Imposing strict global standards that ignore regional operational realities
stakeholders.authority: Owns global IT policy and funding

stakeholders.name: Regional Managers
goals: Ensure compliance with local laws and labor practices
conflicts: Requires customized workflows and data fields
stakeholders.authority: Owns site adoption and can bypass or sabotage corporate software mandates

dependencies.name: Regional Legacy API Extractors
dependencies.description: Extractors to pull data from legacy databases in different locations into the central pipeline.

dependencies.name: Localization Flex-Field Engine
dependencies.description: System config that enables specific UI forms to dynamically show/hide fields based on location.

decisions.decision: Determine global standard vs local flexibility governance rules
decisions.consequence: Delays will cause configuration rework and friction with local managers.

validationQuestions.question: What is the process for a regional manager to request a new custom data field?
Are there specific black-out dates (e.g. holiday sales seasons) for locations during rollout?

rolloutRecommendations.action: Complete data dictionary audit across all regional sites and draft core field schema.
Select pilot location and deploy the core system template with super-user training.
Review pilot feedback and start phased wave deployments across remaining regions.
